Terrible service from Michelle. The visit was not what I expected. Everything was overly discussed and a lot of time spent with a tablet. You see very little of the studios, almost nothing. It's not worth going.

During the premier tour I booked, which lasted over 3 hours, a very good insight into the show business was provided. The tour guide was excellent and overwhelmed his guests with numerous pieces of information. As a Star Trek fan, I found the corresponding props and set pieces (turbolift) particularly interesting, but everything else related to the film business was also fascinating.
Three options are offered online here - a simple two-hour tour, the premier tour lasting three hours (which I booked), and a 4.5-hour VIP tour, which only takes place during the week. The first two options take place daily.
Definitely recommended for cinephiles!

We had a wonderful tour by the best tour guide Chase, who was friendly and charming and full of interesting information. A great experience to see working studios, sets and hear all the background information. We even had ad hoc conversations with various people who’d worked on site for many years, giving us their unique insights and experiences. A great couple of hours, made more delightful by an enthusiastic guide!
